column33: Comments
Fighting moloids and ghost racers with invis woman as a hero led to very intense deck thinning, which powerful but terrible against master strikes :P

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.

column33: Struggled to get the gems working and Black Bolt filled the HQ early which did not synergize with my strategy. AKP gave some KO power that really helped out but I couldn't get rolling until late in the game.

The setup was easier than we thought it was going to be - despite being kind of random the pre selected heroes were pretty versatile. 
Feel like we are testing the scoring formula this week. We decided to let it run til the last turn to maximize focusing for bystanders. We’ll see Wednesday if that was a good strategy or not.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Ehh... This just wasn't fun. Thanos is a pain, Ghost Racers are a pain, Subterranea is somewhat a pain (but at least you can do fun things with them, like fighting moloids extra times thanks to the Space Gem). And being forced to add Thor Corps to your deck when none of the heroes you're required to use have green or blue triggers. Speaking of those heroes, those weren't exactly fun either (Black Bolt ended up being helpful, but I don't like Scarlet Spider). It ended up being fine, I got lucky with my Invisible Woman rare a few robes to stop gems from getting shards and to stop Ghost Racers from resurrecting and causing escapes. Proxima Midnight enjoyed Black Bolt's double icons. I decided I didn't have the mental capacity to play another game, I feel like I didn't do well, but I also think it's possible that others didn't win based on the low power levels of some of the heroes.

Had a really rough start with a handful of higher HO villains which led to a few escapes. I didn't time my rise of the living dead very well, a d it was honestly kind of a messy game. Hopefully I scrape by
With infinity gems not being any victory points it really sucked the life out of the game. Spider Woman did not work as well as I hoped.

In hindsight I probably should've saved these heroes for a harder scheme. Especially since only 1 copy of disappearing act showed up.  I spent a lot of turns in both games with not much to do because of lack of villains to fight and not enough gems showing up to lower Thanos' attack.
I forgot how crazy Gamora's ultimate card is.  Was able to take out Thanos multiple times with just shards alone.
Very fun. Pretty sure we're going to be right at the line again...
Very fun setup, but it was on the easier end. Bribe made Villains easier to fight, and the clones gave some pretty sweet extra recruits
Cocoos + M is my favourite combo, and with sinister clones it goes through the roof, second game gave me Cocoos rare fast, and M rare for few last turn for finishing, it was great

Our first game went great until the last kingpin tactic forced an escape by playing a sinister clone, which then cloned itself. Stacking covert and cloning with m, wasp, and cuckoos went well with starlord and blade as support. Our second attempt ended up taking much longer and we had an escape very early on so we ended up not even bothering finishing because it would not end up quite as good as Game 1. Overall a fun setup.

Clones are fun! I added M to the mix, but she and Warpath did not ended up doing much. Star-Lord and Blade were very good together. However, I lost 2 turns not doing much with them because of Scheme Twists, but they compensate more than enough on other turns.

This setup was enjoyable, especially with the cloning going on. I ran it twice, Both times with the same strategy. One side was Bishop and the Stepfords, while the other side was Domino and Blade. I used Starlord for shards. The undisputed MVP for me was Domino. Versatile is awesome, and her uncommon is insanely strong against Bribe, especially with Chivalrous Duel. Thanks to the Sinister Clones, I was able to clone Element Guns on the other side, which allowed me to generate enough shards to keep the city clear when there was no active twist. 

Kingpin's MS was super frustrating when it hit, and I could have won several turns earlier if not for it hitting. Even though I had Blade on one side, I never managed to have him in my hand when the MS hit.

Really hard to find a groove on this one. Happy when Kingpin played Criminal Empire, it gave me a few extra villains to fight. (Mob war made an odd number of henchmen possible to be in the villain deck) I don't think my hero choices were the best but had fun just the same.

I made the mistake of chasing points with Jean Grey instead of just focusing on winning as quickly as possible. Both of my games were long slogs where combos stubbornly refused to come together. I went with the second attempt as it had the most VP. I had cloned Psychic Search early on and was raking in Bystanders throughout the game, but the power cards needed to really benefit from it didn’t show up until later. I’ll be surprised if I make it through this round.

The early turns saw several villains appear and when the city got full finally my first henchman came in pushing two villains out. Being my second attempt I needed to forge onward to see if I could wrap the game up faster than my first game cause attempt one also saw two escapes from the scheme but took, what felt like at the time, a whopping 38 turns.
Luckily the iceman, bishop, cuckoos train got rolling on the right pretty effectively on this attempt. The cuckoos setup bishop to KO all the starting cards and iceman just launched with all the ranged I was drawing. 
Left side wanted desperately more artifacts in the HQ but alas they did not enter, the only artifact I managed was a single elemental guns. Attempt one ended with about 30 shards on the left side but due to chivalrous shenanigans they were useless and I had to wait an extra four turns to wipe out kingpin because of twists . In attempt two no such engine got going, so the left side didn’t do much in the submitted game.
But it was clear that attempt two was the better result despite having two escapes.
I’d also like to say this is a good example of iceman being a better suited hero for chivalrous duel scenarios than starlord. Both are excellent but in this niche setup Bobby took the cake.
A very average result..

To have Star-Lord but at half mast was rough. Cuckoos and Bishop did went and ramped up quickly with Clone and KO working right away. But had a lot of Chivalrous Duel turns where there could've been MM hits without it. Struggled to really find a good hero to pair with Blade in the setup, and limped in with Jean Grey. I don't think I really got the most out of her inclusion, but c'est la vie! Top moments of the game were pulling Concussive Blast from the Cuckoos investigate card a couple times when I already had the double range trigger.
Professor X's rare card really got everything going and then the other hand had a sweet Invisble Woman KO deck. Really awesome game
Another round of melking

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Great setup! Both decks took off amazingly well. Stryfe’s final tactic put the last 3 master strikes into play! But he still didn’t stand a chance!

Our first game went absolutely horrible. A combination of the twists and strikes coming out quickly really put us under the water and eventually lost due to scheme twists. We decided to put in Iceman instead of cap and it was a much better choice. Wolverine was the MVP this game. Player 2's deck was a little slower due to a lot of his cards being put back into the hero deck from the herald villains. Player 1 was able to get a LOT of card draw and was able to get wolverine's ultimate early in the game while KO'ing all his grey cards. By turn 22, player one was drawing his entire deck each turn, and being able to hit the Mastermind twice each turn. Game was over pretty fast from there on out.

Stryfe is always a good time especially when paired with his always lead the MLF. Honestly, I should have known better than to take Forge for game one. He really needs to show up to be useful. I don't even think I saw his rare. However, Mr. F got me to the point of drawing my whole deck every turn giving me the freedom to clear out of Heralds before finishing off Stryfe still faster than game one.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Fun setup but too easy. Stryfe is not a challenging Mastermind. His tactics are mildly annoying. Human Torch and Invisible Woman were an awesome tag team. I got Nova Flare early so it made the game easy for Player 1.
N/A
Liked this a lot.  The master strikes sure piled up fast though.
Fun game! We had to really strategize when it came to the HQ once we pulled too many scheme twists to try to stay in the game.
The interplay between Professor X's rare and the MANDROIDS is super fun

Fun level of 4/5 is purely due to first attempt. That one went super well and took only 25 turns. Attempt 2 was, as expected, not even close and I lost that one in just 24 turns.

For attempt 1 and hero choices (for both), I made good use of both my choices, Amadeus Cho and Moon Knight. Their Instinct cards also helped out to trigger the Super Power On Mockingbird's 3-cost. Note that only Hand 1 went super well. Hand 2 merely managed to clear the occasional villain from the city and to recruit from HQ what wasn't really good for the other hand.

Deck 1 ended up with Amadeus Cho's 6-cost, one of his 2-costs and a transformed 3-cost (which is a 5-cost), 2 of Mockingbird's 3-cost and some moon knight (a 2 attack common and one uncommon) and some of Hulkbuster Iron Man's 4-costs and K.O.-ing and/or sending undercover all but on of the starters. The end result was a very consistent and powerful deck even early on. I remember defeating Illuminati as early as turn 12, which in attempt 2 took until turn 21.

Deck 2 wasn't really powerful, it began to hope for Symbiote Spider-man, but he rarely appeared, so switched over to some Moon Knight and tried to help out, but barely could.

For the Villain Deck things went pretty well. From the start this setup seemed bery hard, and in attempt 2 it turned out to be so, but thanks to a lucky shuffle I ended up pulling only 4 Scheme Twists and managing to avoid painful Master Strikes. The "drawing cost a discard" effect was annoying, but my deck 1 was good enough to be able to defeat the Illuminati despite their rule. And sometimes it wasn't active, so then I really could do great turns. Outwit was never an issue and only Black Bolt proved unbeatable in the city (I'd rather defeat the Mastermind than him when I managed 13 attack).

Illuminati is is annoying with the discard, but deathlok with his uncommon came through.  The other side had an interesting symbiote spiderman deck which was quite fun.  I had 2 rares, bruce banner and HB ironman rare which worked wonders, especially with deathlok's 2 cost cycling through (at least when it was against the normal illuminati) Overall pretty fun!
Nice setup.  The transformations always found a way to thwart our plans.
Discarding when you draw is no fun! Also having a hero that gets shield clearance from taking shield officers from the stack is not good for this scheme.

First round was a stomp. Scheme Twists came to close together and gave us an unavoidable defeat..
For the second round we focussed und buying/killling scheme officers (and had enough card luck). Towards the end we thought we could'nt get enough damage to the mastermind, 'cause there were 2 Twists waiting for us. So we started playing for a as late as possible defeat. But some player deck draw luck gave us enough damage to defeat 4 masterminds in the last 6 rounds, as well as keeping the scheme officers below 14 for the last two twists. 
The unexpected win felt great, but we hadn't much fun playing this setup: Master Strike timing sucked (one very early), Scheme was hard, Mastermind was op, set heroes were opposing the mastermind very hard, Feast didn't help as well.

Glad we're past this one...

Our first game we could manage the city fine as well as the SHIELD stack but as the HQ got clogged with Rares and we tried to avoid too many SHIELD in our decks we couldn't get enough attack for the MM. There were a few turns that we came up 1 fight short. Then the Scheme hit back to back causing us to lose. 
Our second game we were able to get our decks going fine even though a few villains escaped due to just big villains the first few turns mixed with twist and strikes. The problem here was our twist hit early and back to back again not giving us time to clear the stack.
Spiderman was no help, the scheme/mastermind was good, could not get a way to make heroes to work.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: I hate the Illuminati

column2: Ebony Maw Declares it Opposite Day
column33:
Brought heroes who could help make attck out of recruit, so one side could build an "attack" deck, while the other went with a "recruit deck. Therefore brought Silver Surfer and Thor.

Was extraordinarily lucky in both games, picking up Silver Surfer's rare in attempt 1, and "Pure Fury" in attempt 2. Pure Fury was definitely the better card though. Ended up with a deck of 6 cards on that side at the end of the game. Early strikes(where I only had starters in hand) definitely helped. Think I used up all my luck for the week on this!!

I should have started with my G2 heroes to begin with! G1 I got really lucky with Nick Fury, but G2 felt SO MUCH smoother! I got Silver Surfer's rare and just went nuts while I was in the negative zone.

I was feeling good at the beginning when I could get 10 recruit a turn and hit the mastermind. Then once things switched to needing 15 I was pretty much hopeless. It required significant luck to get there, and I just didn't have it.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Nailed with the 7th scheme twist, had hit Ebony Maw 3 times...oh well!

Call me salty, but I really don't think Epic Masterminds should be used in tournaments like this outside of the Finals. Especially paired with this Scheme, it feels so mean-spirited and doesn't make me look forward to playing that week's setup

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Not having the Sidekicks caused a huge dilemma, and the inopportune times of the twists and the strikes coming out as they did. At least I was able to hold out as long as I did with the final twist arriving to end my chances of winning.

This game really cemented my absolute loathing for Epic Ebony Maw. His Master Strike ability always triggered when I had a rare/ or a pivotal card. That, in combination with the Scheme flip-flopping between Attack/Recruit, led to an aggravating series of matches. First game I thought Invisible Woman and Thor would help KO unwanted cards/rescue some Bystanders and get lots of Recruit but I just seemed to get clogged on their cards. So for game 2 I switched to a strategy of Human Torch for pure power and Captain Marvel for draws. It definitely worked better but I still couldn't pull it off.

What a brutal game. HQ had 4 6 cost heroes at set-up. After 1st recruit, all 6 cost. Got a lucky draw and was able to buy something after my first shuffle. Attack and recruit would swap at the worst time, too. In game 2, I had a pretty lean deck with Mr. Fantastic and Thing. Lost 2 of my twisting equations to Doombots back to back, just to immediately lose both my One Gigantic Hand and Crime Stopper to back to back Master Strikes. I went from a deck that worked, to a deck with 9 cards, 10 recruit and 3 attack. Rebuilding at turn 20 from scratch, just for attack to be used to recruit cards killed us. Bad timing all around.

Very frustrating yet very fun game.  I got enough strikes to hit Ebony but then drew a twist.  So I recruit Thor's rare instead.  Thor's rare finally shows in my hand and I drew a Strike (by by Thor).  My games continued in this way until Ebony Maw destroyed me. But it was still very fun.  I am going to play the scenario more and see if I can get a win...one day.

Soooo many good cards KOd by Maw's Master Strike. Just two really rough games. My second game was actually going ok but unfortunately I had 18 cards left in the VD when I got twist 7 to lose. Just unfortunate luck and also maybe a skill issue. GG to everyone! Another fun tournament despite my performance.
Black Widow go BRRRRRR!!!!!

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: I hope it was fadt enough

I threw in the Stepford coocoos for some cheap heroes, but the cloning really helped and Deadpool’s Oddball put out so much damage with the number of 3 cost heroes I drafted. This was a fun puzzle but I feel I needed a better KO hero as my deck got a little clogged. Wish I had access to XF Wolverine instead of Domino.

Prior to the final turn, we had a whopping 11 bystanders, Then snash played Hawkeye's rare, aquired a few turns prior. With 3 copies of Widow's uncommon in hand, and an almost full city. It was a bloodbath!!
Had to try for bystanders on this set up. I felt heroes had good synergy.

M, Colossus, and Strong Guy was the core of both decks, Hawkeye's draw card was appreciated but other than that the other three heroes didn't pull their weight.  City moved a lot quicker than it did in my first game but I submitted the second for a better VP and turn count.  Hoping that due to heroes in the villain deck that the low VP is enough this week.

Meh. My idea was to try and stick to something with low cost in order to make it more likely to keep Taskmaster's buff and the attack needed to fight heroes in the city down. Didn't really matter, both games just couldn't get a good enough break early on.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Cost synergy worked out pretty well with M and Iron Fist decks. Domino & Colossus were good picks for low cost cards. I doubt I'll be high in the ranking from 1 escape and mid turn count.

The first attempt I forgot to add the 2 bystanders to the villain deck, oops!  Taskmaster is and his thunderbolts are annoying by keep putting the defeated henchmen back into the city.  I'm thinking of playing a 3rd time with this setup and just ignoring the henchmen and never fighting them.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: This was SO thematic! Loved this set up! We brought in rictor to help with XForce and M to rack up some tactical formation combos. Hulk was useful to help KO some of our wounds and bring another avenger in to compliment Hawkeye. Great time!

column2: Mandarin Puts a Ring on a Widow
column33: First game the decks didn't take off fast enough. Second game I had it under control, even avoiding Mandarin's tactic that adds a ring to the escape pile of you have no tech despite no tech heroes by using Fitzsimmons. Then I had a Twist chain end the run.
